* **TOP NEWS**

"**Texas Supreme Court to review Winter Storm Uri claims against utility companies,** " Texas Lawbook's **Janet Elliott** -- "With state electric grid operators eyeing the potential for extreme cold this winter, the [Texas Supreme Court ](https://substack.com/redirect/c9afe96d-75b8-47e4-9678-ba2683523863?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)has agreed to hear a petition from transmission and distribution utility companies seeking dismissal of gross negligence and intentional nuisance claims over devastating winter storm [blackouts](https://substack.com/redirect/afeb5492-4708-4486-a43d-aa535f56bd86?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in February 2021.

Justices will hear arguments Feb. 19, four years after [Winter Storm Uri caused widespread, prolonged power outages](https://substack.com/redirect/b267f4ed-df3d-45e9-88c6-8e0bb9acf581?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) resulting in deaths and property damage.

The court will review an [April decision by a three-judge panel of the Fourteenth Court of Appeals in Houston](https://substack.com/redirect/0beeb299-3df5-478f-8827-47bbe5fd886a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that more than 15,000 plaintiffs may move forward with certain claims in their wrongful death, personal injury and other Uri-related lawsuits seeking billions of dollars in damages.

The Fourteenth Court said the utility companies were entitled to dismissal of the plaintiffs' claims for negligence because Texas law does not impose common-law negligence duties associated with emergency power interruptions, ensuring adequate generation or warning of anticipated power outages.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/de7916c8-8b01-46f4-9472-d9d4754b40bc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
But the court of appeals said that the tariff, which is essentially the state-law mandated contract between customers and the transmission and distribution companies, does allow for gross negligence and intentional nuisance claims.

More than 200 lawsuits, consolidated into multidistrict litigation before Harris County MDL Court Judge **Sylvia Matthews** , allege that mismanagement by companies including Dallas-based Oncor Electric Delivery, CenterPoint Energy in Houston and American Electric Power constituted an intentional nuisance.

Uri was a perfect storm for the electric grid that serves most of Texas as plunging temperatures sparked record high demand amid a severely reduced energy supply, causing the grid to become unbalanced. Beginning at 1:25 a.m. on Feb. 15, 2021, [ERCOT began ordering](https://substack.com/redirect/4cc54bee-47c4-4d7e-aedb-8847c48a20ac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the utilities to cut service to set amounts of customer demand to prevent the grid from collapsing.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/de7916c8-8b01-46f4-9472-d9d4754b40bc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Millions of Texans lost heat and power for several days as the weather remained brutally cold. News reports have attributed nearly 250 deaths to the disaster.

The transmission and distribution companies say they could be the only category of the many entities involved in the crisis to have a legal duty to deliver electricity after court rulings in other Uri-related cases went in favor of natural gas providers, power generators, retail electric providers and ERCOT defendants.

**Michael A. Heidler** , an appellate partner at Vinson & Elkins, said in the companies' petition for review that the Supreme Court should bar the plaintiffs' claims before significant resources are spent adjudicating "15,000 claimants' baseless complaints."

"In these exigent circumstances, no court has imposed common-law duties on TDUs [transmission and distribution utilities] to supply electricity to any particular customers for any particular duration. Established Texas law confirms that courts should not impose such a duty in these circumstances. Plaintiffs' gross-negligence claim should be dismissed," Heidler wrote.

He said the companies did not create a nuisance by shedding load in response to ERCOT's orders. Citing a 2016 Texas Supreme Court pipeline case, Heidler said that "nuisance" is a condition such as smoke, gases or odors that substantially interferes with use and enjoyment of land.

"Here, the 'condition' interfering with use and enjoyment of land was cold air. But it was Mother Nature -- not TDUs -- that blew cold air onto Plaintiffs' properties," Heidler said.

In a response for plaintiff Bernadine Edwards, Dallas lawyer S. Ann Saucer said the utilities are not being sued for complying with ERCOT's load shed orders but because they committed malfeasance that ERCOT did not order.

"TDUs are not being sued because they failed to deliver uninterrupted adequate power but because they, either intentionally or via gross negligence, created part of the power shortfall, told untruths to their customers, and then deprived their misled customers of power for undue stretches of time rather than roll blackouts, with catastrophic results including deaths," said Saucer, who is with Nachawati Law Group.

The utility companies are being supported with briefs from the industry and other business groups." [Texas Lawbook](https://substack.com/redirect/4411910b-bda2-4267-b2e7-8330b5648378?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

($5/mo or $50/yr or $199 lifetime) TOP NEWS "Texas Supreme Court to review Winter Storm Uri claims against utility companies, " Texas Lawbook's Janet Elliott -- "With state electric grid operators eyeing the potential for extreme cold this winter, the agreed to hear a petition from transmission and distribution utility companies seeking dismissal of gross negligence and intentional nuisance claims over devastating winter storm in February 2021. Justices will hear arguments Feb. 19, four years after resulting in deaths and property damage. The court will review an that more than 15,000 plaintiffs may move forward with certain claims in their wrongful death, personal injury and other Uri-related lawsuits seeking billions of dollars in damages. The Fourteenth Court said the utility companies were entitled to dismissal of the plaintiffs' claims for negligence because Texas law does not impose common-law negligence duties associated with emergency power interruptions, ensuring adequate generation or warning of anticipated power outages. But the court of appeals said that the tariff, which is essentially the state-law mandated contract between customers and the transmission and distribution companies, does allow for gross negligence and intentional nuisance claims. More than 200 lawsuits, consolidated into multidistrict litigation before Harris County MDL Court Judge Sylvia Matthews , allege that mismanagement by companies including Dallas-based Oncor Electric Delivery, CenterPoint Energy in Houston and American Electric Power constituted an intentional nuisance. Uri was a perfect storm for the electric grid that serves most of Texas as plunging temperatures sparked record high demand amid a severely reduced energy supply, causing the grid to become unbalanced. Beginning at 1:25 a.m. on Feb. 15, 2021, the utilities to cut service to set amounts of customer demand to prevent the grid from collapsing. Millions of Texans lost heat and power for several days as the weather remained brutally cold. News reports have attributed nearly 250 deaths to the disaster. The transmission and distribution companies say they could be the only category of the many entities involved in the crisis to have a legal duty to deliver electricity after court rulings in other Uri-related cases went in favor of natural gas providers, power generators, retail electric providers and ERCOT defendants. Michael A. Heidler , an appellate partner at Vinson & Elkins, said in the companies' petition for review that the Supreme Court should bar the plaintiffs' claims before significant resources are spent adjudicating "15,000 claimants' baseless complaints." "In these exigent circumstances, no court has imposed common-law duties on TDUs [transmission and distribution utilities] to supply electricity to any particular customers for any particular duration. Established Texas law confirms that courts should not impose such a duty in these circumstances. Plaintiffs' gross-negligence claim should be dismissed," Heidler wrote. He said the companies did not create a nuisance by shedding load in response to ERCOT's orders. Citing a 2016 Texas Supreme Court pipeline case, Heidler said that "nuisance" is a condition such as smoke, gases or odors that substantially interferes with use and enjoyment of land. "Here, the 'condition' interfering with use and enjoyment of land was cold air. But it was Mother Nature -- not TDUs -- that blew cold air onto Plaintiffs' properties," Heidler said. In a response for plaintiff Bernadine Edwards, Dallas lawyer S. Ann Saucer said the utilities are not being sued for complying with ERCOT's load shed orders but because they committed malfeasance that ERCOT did not order. "TDUs are not being sued because they failed to deliver uninterrupted adequate power but because they, either intentionally or via gross negligence, created part of the power shortfall, told untruths to their customers, and then deprived their misled customers of power for undue stretches of time rather than roll blackouts, with catastrophic results including deaths," said Saucer, who is with Nachawati Law Group. The utility companies are being supported with briefs from the industry and other business groups." "**Texas Supreme Court to review Winter Storm Uri claims against utility companies,** " Texas Lawbook's **Janet Elliott** -- "With state electric grid operators eyeing the potential for extreme cold this winter, the [Texas Supreme Court ](https://substack.com/redirect/eb58f019-66a4-45cd-b412-25a4fc159f78?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)has agreed to hear a petition from transmission and distribution utility companies seeking dismissal of gross negligence and intentional nuisance claims over devastating winter storm [blackouts](https://substack.com/redirect/6fdf8c0d-559c-42c1-a619-4b2b36b814ad?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in February 2021.

Justices will hear arguments Feb. 19, four years after [Winter Storm Uri caused widespread, prolonged power outages](https://substack.com/redirect/7c77b5c2-7eba-4095-b000-51f3852f2521?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) resulting in deaths and property damage.

The court will review an [April decision by a three-judge panel of the Fourteenth Court of Appeals in Houston](https://substack.com/redirect/d02bafa5-15cf-413c-bfb5-050407734385?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that more than 15,000 plaintiffs may move forward with certain claims in their wrongful death, personal injury and other Uri-related lawsuits seeking billions of dollars in damages.

The Fourteenth Court said the utility companies were entitled to dismissal of the plaintiffs' claims for negligence because Texas law does not impose common-law negligence duties associated with emergency power interruptions, ensuring adequate generation or warning of anticipated power outages.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/8432577a-cc09-46b6-9b92-417b86ad7f6e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
But the court of appeals said that the tariff, which is essentially the state-law mandated contract between customers and the transmission and distribution companies, does allow for gross negligence and intentional nuisance claims.

More than 200 lawsuits, consolidated into multidistrict litigation before Harris County MDL Court Judge **Sylvia Matthews** , allege that mismanagement by companies including Dallas-based Oncor Electric Delivery, CenterPoint Energy in Houston and American Electric Power constituted an intentional nuisance.

Uri was a perfect storm for the electric grid that serves most of Texas as plunging temperatures sparked record high demand amid a severely reduced energy supply, causing the grid to become unbalanced. Beginning at 1:25 a.m. on Feb. 15, 2021, [ERCOT began ordering](https://substack.com/redirect/59f37bf7-66b5-4887-9981-00ad4cae9ec3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the utilities to cut service to set amounts of customer demand to prevent the grid from collapsing.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/8432577a-cc09-46b6-9b92-417b86ad7f6e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Millions of Texans lost heat and power for several days as the weather remained brutally cold. News reports have attributed nearly 250 deaths to the disaster.

The transmission and distribution companies say they could be the only category of the many entities involved in the crisis to have a legal duty to deliver electricity after court rulings in other Uri-related cases went in favor of natural gas providers, power generators, retail electric providers and ERCOT defendants.

**Michael A. Heidler** , an appellate partner at Vinson & Elkins, said in the companies' petition for review that the Supreme Court should bar the plaintiffs' claims before significant resources are spent adjudicating "15,000 claimants' baseless complaints."

"In these exigent circumstances, no court has imposed common-law duties on TDUs [transmission and distribution utilities] to supply electricity to any particular customers for any particular duration. Established Texas law confirms that courts should not impose such a duty in these circumstances. Plaintiffs' gross-negligence claim should be dismissed," Heidler wrote.

He said the companies did not create a nuisance by shedding load in response to ERCOT's orders. Citing a 2016 Texas Supreme Court pipeline case, Heidler said that "nuisance" is a condition such as smoke, gases or odors that substantially interferes with use and enjoyment of land.

"Here, the 'condition' interfering with use and enjoyment of land was cold air. But it was Mother Nature -- not TDUs -- that blew cold air onto Plaintiffs' properties," Heidler said.

In a response for plaintiff Bernadine Edwards, Dallas lawyer S. Ann Saucer said the utilities are not being sued for complying with ERCOT's load shed orders but because they committed malfeasance that ERCOT did not order.

"TDUs are not being sued because they failed to deliver uninterrupted adequate power but because they, either intentionally or via gross negligence, created part of the power shortfall, told untruths to their customers, and then deprived their misled customers of power for undue stretches of time rather than roll blackouts, with catastrophic results including deaths," said Saucer, who is with Nachawati Law Group.

The utility companies are being supported with briefs from the industry and other business groups." [Texas Lawbook](https://substack.com/redirect/dfff4a72-35c6-4b87-9ab1-eb638f3434cb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

"TDUs are not being sued because they failed to deliver uninterrupted adequate power but because they, either intentionally or via gross negligence, created part of the power shortfall, told untruths to their customers, and then deprived their misled customers of power for undue stretches of time rather than roll blackouts, with catastrophic results including deaths," said Saucer, who is with Nachawati Law Group. The utility companies are being supported with briefs from the industry and other business groups." FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. Free subscribers will only receive the top story in their morning email. * **TOP NEWS**

“**Texas Supreme Court to review Winter Storm Uri claims against utility companies,** ” Texas Lawbook's **Janet Elliott** — “With state electric grid operators eyeing the potential for extreme cold this winter, the [Texas Supreme Court ](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eOQPXJLmqZGCIlkWp25_d_kyV1jVlWk3CTz_vgAsQdzmBgufWxc5kd4eOi8KJgGoqVDNDPdYlhjRJR2wziXSq0yyrzAUfmhhf0KTzRLyHuzWj-5kPYTh1veWKv-_DFHYuTi1bMDL14omhdiR5EUYX1Ki8M3IXuPU2EukZukjdADsmKKKrX62IOUU49TNbc58OLd6ka9buBYd2sd2kocAKnk61mqhhNv0-8ZjzqxdecoppRkaIj5kxG7XEL-pPpput0HvSHP7cG5dndagUF6fT3P4F9iI1IufcTp9kTbyrAN030BXXrF7eYrKCHilUDIv1jWAkcyrmxFZhdEwV3N-cig/4cu/Zm-WAgHFQHiJKOcMJwjzVg/h10/h001.WKcokH6w4NA8wP1joBvdVqIcsjM7E7feMOh4HhEzzt0)has agreed to hear a petition from transmission and distribution utility companies seeking dismissal of gross negligence and intentional nuisance claims over devastating winter storm [blackouts](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eOQPXJLmqZGCIlkWp25_d_mInsAfwXwmf7PaQzj6bqkXNsuHaa5mx1tjOfVdK3YZstrQcYdRauZErqsjhKD7x4TzkfEu2VdkQBAmtj_0Y4D07wV7E8qzU3AU5mRYZQauvwTbp50ZAwBAXPQKhgcHkR8zbPmaV4n1AgYw7ALNsM4Ob4h4ACZ3sr5pr4PT6RncI0K4po1-HtNtPldOYr19avQ-N0O9gG51UiAMCjaq5fKd57U_KRX1LsUD9LmPZpYjTFvwdmsEh8zB7rtVUFmt5UQqlZpex9EHickztKQRHPKh-yaKwKlazHqiYWMpDqodhws8HLm6Pqb-L07aHejD-priWdajH5Ev4-Kll98h4IvE/4cu/Zm-WAgHFQHiJKOcMJwjzVg/h11/h001.1Jv6a8bvDrgYPihW-QLrVQumKLeEVwEo6UIpIKxB1GQ) in February 2021.

Justices will hear arguments Feb. 19, four years after [Winter Storm Uri caused widespread, prolonged power outages](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eOQPXJLmqZGCIlkWp25_d_mRfrsNGXltMxC1ltSg0Zb1oeN-s8BhnqptJgajqTYa92sXKppjxMUFDMg6QfugEDpgyt6gU_8gcz4VSpDajU4pQ7LIwuU6N1iLeXM9UsJ2pZ7uoqRLD1y1fWxWWkf6uOFpCETKtODUB39Vh7VctfocEsfWJJKKRJ-toTyX72aIg-5rXzzU_Lh-djkbyh5k2RSr__9YdXJDiNhOgioJqmkQUthshboDtY1U1fXA-KdkH-OFI05pOJFNAIubK0FEDI7kNNWEA-M7oOK6krTA1clJrn-IILddP5JNq-57oEWe7lZxk2xIv4Z0AdIGrOheKgZuWq2Mpd3lUJ6WaVmLOzZmlqbUR-7hJzxlrQKur5HZ0w/4cu/Zm-WAgHFQHiJKOcMJwjzVg/h12/h001.JT2h1E5s5vNqZjbqVVIsBsc7S1QmA1tdSEi3FSFWhJ0) resulting in deaths and property damage.

The court will review an [April decision by a three-judge panel of the Fourteenth Court of Appeals in Houston](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eOQPXJLmqZGCIlkWp25_d_kyV1jVlWk3CTz_vgAsQdzm0GDOEpQfTwwfwVAbe2JzKeR2MoBaO2Y7Ou6NJ8beST6hNNJgh6vY-RcOqpGuI_TNnSVHtkThL2gIaiX3h6o7EmAeTJxMoWDQJIHnrmVpvqbZXFWD9BEhwHkn5_egLwkpQ8Pr_KCZAZot_9-pJ2ViW3RNSyN_mRq6hZkUZYP0GYvZlSCiqZqrT-5fOG20ihDguEcHODTSqFbQq255_65kRlZPI9ARthCXirARPjjJbgob-ATkN5D_3dH_qS5J7JV3RdQFjYBFn8h0Nq65Ewdg62mLansVsMBReDOXF34tUkJrXWSKl_hz81yIAaStfPdL/4cu/Zm-WAgHFQHiJKOcMJwjzVg/h13/h001.bt3OgnNkR3NKV_9PSDx317uhe-AkbJyAHxShqa4mnWM) that more than 15,000 plaintiffs may move forward with certain claims in their wrongful death, personal injury and other Uri-related lawsuits seeking billions of dollars in damages.

The Fourteenth Court said the utility companies were entitled to dismissal of the plaintiffs’ claims for negligence because Texas law does not impose common-law negligence duties associated with emergency power interruptions, ensuring adequate generation or warning of anticipated power outages.
» [BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.IpGzAqnc3m6dMmKQdod4OdZdsfX-sjnRfDuEmaXW-5lN-IqpP1xu2_Ygevf9CBs8iGkDwdDuKv-OIdRCcryDDgoqnwbBfsyGSk_4VJrOSCvmgxZEZTEXv72w0U68JXHpny88FhFoE4mM3a9phL8bbDcJugj8I6ME43MOhjpIhZOtBOFppit_BqhHsI5Y_sAr0SalQiX6wCx7iuyFzHgZJ_haAK_I-hddo__xTMN7_oE/4cu/Zm-WAgHFQHiJKOcMJwjzVg/h14/h001.pCMFm_C9pXR4-qV1sJl0pKbXCW82RfzpVot29MvBqiE)
But the court of appeals said that the tariff, which is essentially the state-law mandated contract between customers and the transmission and distribution companies, does allow for gross negligence and intentional nuisance claims.

More than 200 lawsuits, consolidated into multidistrict litigation before Harris County MDL Court Judge **Sylvia Matthews** , allege that mismanagement by companies including Dallas-based Oncor Electric Delivery, CenterPoint Energy in Houston and American Electric Power constituted an intentional nuisance.

Uri was a perfect storm for the electric grid that serves most of Texas as plunging temperatures sparked record high demand amid a severely reduced energy supply, causing the grid to become unbalanced. Beginning at 1:25 a.m. on Feb. 15, 2021, [ERCOT began ordering](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eOQPXJLmqZGCIlkWp25_d_lcmyP8m6JFNdKqfGOtsiVsN-RKJ3LUx99_tvdu1k26HFEk0t3mgEngBsv5hiVqQfdZIdw39lzF_LnRhhUc4Ot1Qp7UEH6rNfuKadTtBorPHR5Y5pa96kYiOkYoB-rwf6lPcoP9Nj4KgmjexNBwYUZP1gySXr9VxmB-504wmEEDI6x6MCHi3xRwjZt0vKvywNiOago9zBpBpwbjiQdZfnugs5wPz742tOC4Gclhgk35XjM2iB6G9SXjcfebUlaoWA5asRzDh3jbdtGaXKMdPM62WjtGq-dWBesvLvpnLFRbFw/4cu/Zm-WAgHFQHiJKOcMJwjzVg/h15/h001.pg3U0F5hK8hPZ7Ka7dbtpqWUiNx9v8lI1kBPfi16cu4) the utilities to cut service to set amounts of customer demand to prevent the grid from collapsing.
» [BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.IpGzAqnc3m6dMmKQdod4OdZdsfX-sjnRfDuEmaXW-5lN-IqpP1xu2_Ygevf9CBs8iGkDwdDuKv-OIdRCcryDDgoqnwbBfsyGSk_4VJrOSCvmgxZEZTEXv72w0U68JXHpny88FhFoE4mM3a9phL8bbDcJugj8I6ME43MOhjpIhZMm5dCW7Y8BOGaKl-yDP4DxJWjdyv2MtD_mG0H4fhV-_1fX7gi3IpJs-qVhjY4X6WI/4cu/Zm-WAgHFQHiJKOcMJwjzVg/h16/h001.Q1k3pqVohjGNvI9Vo0ka3EE0DTG184gAxTYqPk5fqao)
Millions of Texans lost heat and power for several days as the weather remained brutally cold. News reports have attributed nearly 250 deaths to the disaster.

The transmission and distribution companies say they could be the only category of the many entities involved in the crisis to have a legal duty to deliver electricity after court rulings in other Uri-related cases went in favor of natural gas providers, power generators, retail electric providers and ERCOT defendants.

**Michael A. Heidler** , an appellate partner at Vinson & Elkins, said in the companies’ petition for review that the Supreme Court should bar the plaintiffs’ claims before significant resources are spent adjudicating “15,000 claimants’ baseless complaints.”

“In these exigent circumstances, no court has imposed common-law duties on TDUs [transmission and distribution utilities] to supply electricity to any particular customers for any particular duration. Established Texas law confirms that courts should not impose such a duty in these circumstances. Plaintiffs’ gross-negligence claim should be dismissed,” Heidler wrote.

He said the companies did not create a nuisance by shedding load in response to ERCOT’s orders. Citing a 2016 Texas Supreme Court pipeline case, Heidler said that “nuisance” is a condition such as smoke, gases or odors that substantially interferes with use and enjoyment of land.

“Here, the ‘condition’ interfering with use and enjoyment of land was cold air. But it was Mother Nature — not TDUs — that blew cold air onto Plaintiffs’ properties,” Heidler said.

In a response for plaintiff Bernadine Edwards, Dallas lawyer S. Ann Saucer said the utilities are not being sued for complying with ERCOT’s load shed orders but because they committed malfeasance that ERCOT did not order.

“TDUs are not being sued because they failed to deliver uninterrupted adequate power but because they, either intentionally or via gross negligence, created part of the power shortfall, told untruths to their customers, and then deprived their misled customers of power for undue stretches of time rather than roll blackouts, with catastrophic results including deaths,” said Saucer, who is with Nachawati Law Group.

The utility companies are being supported with briefs from the industry and other business groups." ($5/mo or $50/yr or $199 lifetime) | TOP NEWS “Texas Supreme Court to review Winter Storm Uri claims against utility companies, ” Texas Lawbook's Janet Elliott — “With state electric grid operators eyeing the potential for extreme cold this winter, the agreed to hear a petition from transmission and distribution utility companies seeking dismissal of gross negligence and intentional nuisance claims over devastating winter storm in February 2021. Justices will hear arguments Feb. 19, four years after resulting in deaths and property damage. The court will review an that more than 15,000 plaintiffs may move forward with certain claims in their wrongful death, personal injury and other Uri-related lawsuits seeking billions of dollars in damages. The Fourteenth Court said the utility companies were entitled to dismissal of the plaintiffs’ claims for negligence because Texas law does not impose common-law negligence duties associated with emergency power interruptions, ensuring adequate generation or warning of anticipated power outages. » But the court of appeals said that the tariff, which is essentially the state-law mandated contract between customers and the transmission and distribution companies, does allow for gross negligence and intentional nuisance claims. More than 200 lawsuits, consolidated into multidistrict litigation before Harris County MDL Court Judge Sylvia Matthews , allege that mismanagement by companies including Dallas-based Oncor Electric Delivery, CenterPoint Energy in Houston and American Electric Power constituted an intentional nuisance. Uri was a perfect storm for the electric grid that serves most of Texas as plunging temperatures sparked record high demand amid a severely reduced energy supply, causing the grid to become unbalanced. Beginning at 1:25 a.m. on Feb. 15, 2021, the utilities to cut service to set amounts of customer demand to prevent the grid from collapsing. » Millions of Texans lost heat and power for several days as the weather remained brutally cold. News reports have attributed nearly 250 deaths to the disaster. The transmission and distribution companies say they could be the only category of the many entities involved in the crisis to have a legal duty to deliver electricity after court rulings in other Uri-related cases went in favor of natural gas providers, power generators, retail electric providers and ERCOT defendants. Michael A. Heidler , an appellate partner at Vinson & Elkins, said in the companies’ petition for review that the Supreme Court should bar the plaintiffs’ claims before significant resources are spent adjudicating “15,000 claimants’ baseless complaints.” “In these exigent circumstances, no court has imposed common-law duties on TDUs [transmission and distribution utilities] to supply electricity to any particular customers for any particular duration. Established Texas law confirms that courts should not impose such a duty in these circumstances. Plaintiffs’ gross-negligence claim should be dismissed,” Heidler wrote. He said the companies did not create a nuisance by shedding load in response to ERCOT’s orders. Citing a 2016 Texas Supreme Court pipeline case, Heidler said that “nuisance” is a condition such as smoke, gases or odors that substantially interferes with use and enjoyment of land. “Here, the ‘condition’ interfering with use and enjoyment of land was cold air. But it was Mother Nature — not TDUs — that blew cold air onto Plaintiffs’ properties,” Heidler said. In a response for plaintiff Bernadine Edwards, Dallas lawyer S. Ann Saucer said the utilities are not being sued for complying with ERCOT’s load shed orders but because they committed malfeasance that ERCOT did not order. “TDUs are not being sued because they failed to deliver uninterrupted adequate power but because they, either intentionally or via gross negligence, created part of the power shortfall, told untruths to their customers, and then deprived their misled customers of power for undue stretches of time rather than roll blackouts, with catastrophic results including deaths,” said Saucer, who is with Nachawati Law Group. The utility companies are being supported with briefs from the industry and other business groups." | FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. | [](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=9c74da937e2b474496ee6887f5095f73&_e=NC1vklItHRfLRzux15kQO-ER1BENJteVW-gWUFVIhwekNYKkHcnQ1ivinGTx5xbTMnTgLcGiYhdaN8tiom6EVO_c2amgbsdFEfRcNLYdlilrzhy3bWTyhUyidNxSFfFVFbg-hqCYIWrSMpq4bvcNWNjV5N_1GUCaHNtTPxKFK61c695s9VHGEE0SY56fXGgAgjX05Hhe1thxxqXENgBOYfX_soIxb2pBcdQuhpKMN4pSwKs1lFik4YuEtKtcjMb-mNddsrnqfWjHZhe_qbYY7x8cuFCGDzTRE6153vKjjkVsAw4ZAKYLU5IMLwxNCmYTH5qMuzojszDjFyJlrpIcOBUQc-kcvuQy-2HUEu3VH1Az1694uMIF_tiHTVgQ_XGp)

Dear Friend,
---
When Donald J. Trump is inaugurated as our 47th President of the United States, he will already be facing a _$486 MILLION_ witch hunt against him in the state of New York.
---
**_Fox News_ : “New York AG James says she won’t drop civil fraud case against Trump”**
---
And every day that passes, President Trump will incur a _$112,000 daily fine_ , as Tish James continues her legal warfare to bleed the President of the United States dry.
---
**Not only is James ignoring the _REAL_ crimes taking place all over New York, she’s choosing to weaken the President at a time when our nation is in serious decline.**
---
Radical Leftists like Tish James do not care about seeing America succeed and prosper. They only care about seeing their political enemies destroyed.
---
Our Republic cannot survive if our justice system continues to be hijacked by legal warfare and political vengeance.
---
There is only one solution – _and that is to FIRE Tish James from her position as Attorney General._
---
**I am running to fire Tish James and end the politicization of the Office of Attorney General – but to take on the massive New York special interests who have weaponized our legal system, I’m going to need support from grassroots patriots like _YOU_ all across America.**
---

clean_body: We plan to give this to President Trump on January 20th Our phones have been ringing off the hook for the past week for the chance to sign President Trump's Inaugural Congratulatory Card. We plan to give this to President Trump on the day of his inauguration, congratulating him on becoming the 47th President. However, as his BEST supporter, we figured your name should be ON TOP. You better act fast, because the opportunity to be on TOP closes at midnight. AND, lucky for you - we decided to extend the opportunity to sign to the END OF THE WEEK... HURRY before you don't get a chance to sign it AT ALL. ******************************************************************************************************** If you want to help Republicans KEEP THE SENATE MAJORITY, text WIN to 55404 to receive mobile alerts! name: Leah Donahey, LCV
subject: (TAKE ACTION) Tell the Biden Administration to STOP LNG Exports from Raising Costs and Polluting Our Air
body:
To view this mail in a browser, copy https://links.messages.lcv.org/servlet/MailView?ms=MTE5NzAwMTMS1&r=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&j=MjE0MDA1MTI0OQS2&mt=1&rt=0 into your browser.
Peter: https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/EZ_ Tell the Biden Administration:

Stop Dirty Methane Gas Exports A new analysis from The Department of Energy shows that liquified methane gas
(LNG) exports would raise energy costs and escalate climate change.

We need your help: President Biden has the chance to reject additional LNG
export projects now building on this new information. But he needs to hear from you if that’s going to happen.

Please take action right now — it only takes a moment to help us reach our 5,000 signature goal >> https://links.messages.lcv. https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/G8bxcK98q_ Peter — A newly released study shows that new liquified methane gas
(LNG) export facilities would increaseenergy prices and escalate the climate crisis, all while oil and gas executives
get richer. President Biden has a chance to block additional projects — but we
need your help to make sure that happens. The research is clear: LNG is a bad bet for the economy and harmful for the environment.But fossil fuel lobbyists continue to boost their profits by increasing energy
prices for families and pushing through even more climate-harming methane emissions. We need your help to make sure that environmentalists’ voices are heard before the Biden administration leaves office. Help us reach 5,000 signatures demanding that President Biden secure his legacy by protecting our communities from harmful methane gas and higher energy costs >> https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/vNR6fEXPa- TAKE ACTION >> https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/04wrM8_32~ For far too long, Big Oil executives have pushed to expand LNG exports,
prioritizing their profits over our climate and our health. Export capacity is
already set to triple from projects that have already been approved. These exports will increase energy costs, increase climate-harming emissions,
and wreak havoc on communities already overburdened by pollution. We simply cannot afford more climate-polluting LNG projects. Please don’t wait — take action right now. Tell President Biden to REJECT dirty methane gas exports while there’s still time. It only takes a moment to help us reach our 5,000 petition signature goal >> https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/L6b_sLRaW- Thanks for taking action, Peter. Onward, Leah Donahey
Senior Federal Advocacy Campaigns Director
League of Conservation Voters This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you,update your contact information https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/ContactInformation?spMailingID=11970013&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MjE0MDA1MTI0OQS2&spReportId=MjE0MDA1MTI0OQS2.Click here to unsubscribe https://www.pages08.net/leagueofconservationvoters/forms/Unsubscribe?spMailingID=11970013&spUserID=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&spJobID=MjE0MDA1MTI0OQS2&spReportId=MjE0MDA1MTI0OQS2 from our supporter list.Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback here mailto:feedback@lcv.org?subject=, or just reply to this email! Thanks for your support.

740 15th St NW, Suite 700
Washington, DC 20005
202-785-8683 DONATE https://links.messages.lcv.

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/qasimrashid/p/americas-hell-corporations?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Thirty days ago, on December 4, the UnitedHealth CEO was allegedly killed by Luigi Mangione. Corporate media has focused heavily on the sensational details of this case. But in my view there's an even larger question too few seem to be asking: Since the CEO's death, how many preventable deaths have occurred because of the exploitative policies of health insurance companies like UnitedHealth, and where is the accountability for those deaths? The answer is daunting, disgusting, and destructive to America's peace and security--yet corporate media continues to ignore it. **Let 's Address This.**
**We need all hands on deck in 2025 to stand against fascism and disinformation. I invite you to join our movement for justice as a free or paid subscriber.**
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack..qBla8jROV8sRgr4uguABKHwG6jf2CP76z_UM_EaD3NY?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=154089406)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/81302bf6-dc81-4d9a-b853-6ba1f7d09ee8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
(L) Luigi Mangione is accused of killing (R) UnitedHealth CEO Brian Thompson.
According to _The Lancet_ , America's for-profit health system is responsible for nearly **68,000 preventable deaths every year** --that's about **5,589 preventable deaths** in the 30 days since the CEO's killing. If we agree killing is wrong, and Mangione is on trial because he is accused of killing the CEO, then the next question is simple: When do the CEOs who knowingly enabled these 5589 deaths go on trial? UnitedHealth is already being sued for enabling the deaths of senior citizens for knowingly denying 90% of patients the critical medications they needed to live. But beyond civil lawsuits, when do the criminal trials begin for knowingly causing these thousands of deaths?
The answer? They never will. Because in America, we've normalized corporate greed and profit-driven healthcare policies as "business as usual," even when they kill tens of thousands of people annually. And those responsible? They remain a protected class.
### **What 's Considered a Crime? It Depends on Who You Are**
If you want to understand how crime is a social construct in America, consider these cases:
* **Briana Boston** , a mother of three in Florida with no criminal record, send to her insurance corporation BlueCrossBlue Shield _" delay, defend, deny you're next," _after they denied her healthcare claim. She owns no weapons, committed no act of violence, but was arrested, charged with terrorism, and had to pay a $100,000 bond to get out of jail. She now faces 15 years in prison for terrorism.
* **Brad Spafford** , a white man in Virginia, was found this week with multiple illegal guns, 150 illegal home made bombs, had openly advocated for the assassination of Vice President Kamala Harris, blew off three of his own fingers while testing his weapons, and lied to the FBI about his weapons and bombs. He has still not been charged with terrorism and only had to pay a $25,000 bond to be released.

Let's be clear: Spafford had actual bombs and guns with intent to use them, yet isn't labeled a terrorist, and faces only a minor weapons charge. Meanwhile, Boston stated empty words that insurance companies didn't like and is being charged with terrorism.
This double standard is glaring. It shows us exactly how power is wielded in America.
Violence committed by the wealthy and powerful, even when it results in death, is normalized. But when everyday people speak out against exploitation, they're met with the full force of the legal system.
### **Class Warfare: Who Gets to Be a Protected Class?**
If you think this discrepancy is unique, let's take it a step further:
* **Dylann Roof** , a white supremacist who killed nine Black people at a church in Charleston, South Carolina, with the stated goal of starting a race war, was not charged with terrorism.
* **Luigi Mangione** , who killed one CEO in what he described as a rebuke of America's exploitative healthcare system, is charged with terrorism.

What's the difference? Simple: wealthy CEOs are a protected class, and working Black people are not. A juxtaposition that makes no sense, yet is the ground reality.
I can give multiple more examples, but they all point to the painful truth that our systems of power only seem to define violence and terrorism when it is directed at the wealthy and powerful--not when the wealthy and powerful enact policies that literally kill or ensure the death of working people.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..qBla8jROV8sRgr4uguABKHwG6jf2CP76z_UM_EaD3NY?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=154089406)
### **The Real Violence We Refuse to Acknowledge**
No, I don't think killing CEOs is the answer. But I do think we need to recognize that the violence of America's healthcare system is real and ongoing. Nearly 70,000 Americans die preventable deaths each year because health insurance corporations prioritize profit over care.
That is violence.
Denying care to someone who needs life-saving medication is violence. Forcing families into medical bankruptcy is violence. Designing policies that knowingly cause thousands of preventable deaths is violence.
But because we've normalized this violence, we don't even acknowledge it as such. And if we don't call out this systemic violence for what it is, then we will never make progress toward protecting ourselves from future harm. Because it isn't just about demanding accountability for the nearly 70,000 Americans who died in the past year because health insurance corporations were more focused on raising profits to "improve shareholder value," it is about preventing the atrocity of another nearly 70,000 Americans who will die over the next year because health insurance corporations will continue to prioritize profits over people.
### **It 's Time to Stop Calling Them 'Health Insurance Companies'**
You might be wondering why I titled this article with "America's HELL Corporations?" It isn't just a play on words. It's because we must stop using the sanitized term "health insurance companies" to describe what these corporations actually do.
These are not benevolent entities providing care--physicians and nurses provide the care. They are not insuring health either--we insure our own health with exercise and healthy eating. Instead, they are exploiting illness. And then, after denying us access to the care we need when we get hurt or sick as all people do, they limit their own liability to prevent meaningful accountability. Therefore, I propose going forward we stop using "health insurance corporations" because the term is inaccurate and false.
Instead, call them what they are: Health Exploitation Limited Liability Corporations.
Or, more succinctly: HELL Corporations.
Because that's what they create--hell--for millions of Americans forced to fight for basic care, navigate endless bureaucracy, and pay exorbitant prices for life-saving treatments--which are then all too often denied without accountability. And by the way, these 70,000 deaths do not account for the tens of millions more who suffer in agony and pain, receiving just enough care to not die, but not enough to regain health. That preventable but forced suffering is violence. It is HELL. And violent hell demands accountability.
So let's call them what they are: HELL Corporations.
[Give a gift subscription](https://substack..qBla8jROV8sRgr4uguABKHwG6jf2CP76z_UM_EaD3NY?&gift=true)
### **The Call to Action: Stand Against Corporate Exploitation**
In the 30 days since the UnitedHealth CEO was killed, nearly 6000 Americans have died due to lack of healthcare access due to these exploitative HELL Corporations. And 30 days from now, some 6000 more Americans will have died a similar fate. And I am willing to bet this is likely the first time you heard this horrifying fact so much as acknowledged by any corporate media outlet. This is the goal of HELL Corporations, and the failure of corporate media.
Corporate media will never hold HELL Corporations accountable. They will continue to whitewash the exploitation, downplay the systemic violence, and protect the powerful. That's why independent voices are so essential in this fight for justice.
If this resonated with you, subscribe to this newsletter and join us in speaking truth to power. Together, we can amplify the stories that corporate media refuses to tell. We can demand accountability. And we can keep fighting for a healthcare system that values human life over corporate profit. United, we can stop the vice grip of HELL Corporations, and demand healthcare as a human right for all people in this country.
Let's keep going. Let's hold the powerful accountable. And let's never stop calling out injustice.

name: Josh Harder
subject: I just took the Oath of Office!
body:
Guess who joined me?

| | Hey Peter, Today, I was officially sworn into the 119th Congress! This year I had some special guests with me - Pam, our two daughters Lillian and Karina, and my parents Linda and Mark joined, too. I don’t take this opportunity for granted and it was really amazing to share this moment with them. _A front-row seat to see democracy in action!_ Look, I know the national backdrop for today’s swearing-in. The next few years are going to be difficult, but we’ve worked too hard to throw up our hands. I recognize how much of a responsibility this job is and how many people have put their faith in me. I also know we are in a tenuous time for our country, and it’s times like these when we need good people to stay in the fight. That means me and that certainly means this team. **Here’s my promise to you: I promise to work my heart out to defend our freedoms. I promise to do my best to make our world a better, safer place for our kids. And I promise I’ll never forget what this team sent me here to do.** If you want to help me continue fighting for our families in Congress, you can use my link here to make a donation: **[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/Josh2025](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99800410/520909307/-31595347?refcode=E11784-20250103-SwearingIn-SL-Other&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDMxMTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOGNiZGI1YzMtMDBjYS1lZjExLTg4ZDAtMDAyMjQ4MmE5YjQ1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=VdEGWPSFvXS-OfpzCtckSzzzHZETfI62oQkEPAn5A60=)** Thank you for being on this journey with me, I won’t let you down. \- Josh
---
* * *
You are receiving this email because Congressman Josh Harder is one of our last lines of defense to protect our democracy against MAGA extremists like Donald Trump and Marjorie Taylor Greene. [**Josh doesn't take a dime of corporate PAC money, so please chip in now to our campaign!**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99800411/520909308/1618730853?refcode=E11784-20250103-SwearingIn-footer-Other&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDMxMTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOGNiZGI1YzMtMDBjYS1lZjExLTg4ZDAtMDAyMjQ4MmE5YjQ1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=VdEGWPSFvXS-OfpzCtckSzzzHZETfI62oQkEPAn5A60=) [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99800412/520909309/1618730853?refcode=E11784-20250103-SwearingIn-footer-Other&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDMxMTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOGNiZGI1YzMtMDBjYS1lZjExLTg4ZDAtMDAyMjQ4MmE5YjQ1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=VdEGWPSFvXS-OfpzCtckSzzzHZETfI62oQkEPAn5A60=) If you would like to receive fewer emails, we understand. name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: I hold the Uvalde parents who lost their children in the Robb Elementary massacre in 2022 in the very highest esteem.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE
body:
[1]Powered By People

I hold the Uvalde parents who lost their children in the Robb Elementary
massacre in 2022 in the very highest esteem.

They’ve experienced what is, for most of us, unimaginable tragedy. And
instead of shrinking from the world or the politics that have so badly
failed them, they have engaged at a level beyond what most other Texans
would be willing to.

That has included advocating publicly for improved gun laws and school
safety, meeting with lawmakers at the state and federal level, testifying
in nationally broadcast hearings and even running for political office.

Now the Uvalde parents are hosting an exhibit of photos in Austin to honor
the Uvalde victims and their families, and continue the necessary
conversations about gun violence. They hope to put faces and a compelling
emotional connection to the number one killer of children in America -
guns.

Amy and I have made a contribution to help underwrite this exhibit and
provide support to the Uvalde families and their fight for common-sense
gun legislation. I hope you will too.

Link to donate: [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/63887?t=1001&akid=239937%2E444211%2EWYupGd ]secure.everyaction.com/FR-ZvC_WLk2jE1Ayon-Z-w2

Thank you,

Beto O’Rourke

The exhibit will be held in Austin, TX at Canopy Projects Gallery, January
10 - January 19.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1002&akid=239937%2E444211%2EWYupGd

PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE

name: Troy Downing
subject: I was JUST sworn in
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Troy Downing for Congress
body:
I’m already thinking about the road ahead

| | | [](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)
---
Peter, I was JUST sworn into the 119th Congress, and I couldn’t be more grateful for all your support!

Looking at the road ahead and the fight for our Nation's future, I need your help more than ever. With your support, we can defeat the radical Left and hand over a prosperous America to our future generations.

The real fight starts now, and to succeed in this mission, I’ll need you to help me hit the ground running. **[**Will you chip in and be my FIRST official donor as a Congressman?**](https://trk.emails.troydowning.-ng)**
---
[ BE TROY'S FIRST OFFICIAL DONOR](https://trk.emails.troydowning.-L8FZtGEth0V7YMPU0CQQ)
---
I look forward to everything we will accomplish together this year.

Thanks again,
Congressman Troy Downing
---

---
In 5 days, all fundraising will have to stop.
In 5 days, I will focus entirely on passing meaningful legislation to make Maryland the best state it can be.
Before the legislative session gavels in, we need to make the next 5 days count, friend. With that being said, we’re setting a **$17,000 goal** to help us hit the mark and start the session on a high note.
[**So what do you say, friend, will you chip in $5, $10, $25, or whatever you can today? Any amount will help us continue to make this Maryland’s decade.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99801021/520910864/-829868801?refcode=em20250103-1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMGZhMmIwNC0wM2NhLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTliNDUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mPiklJE4jPc2PBSI5eMMcyOYDPdE_3owcTlkWzJLj_4=)

clean_body: Friend, I hope you had a Happy New Year! Can you believe that the fundraising freeze starts in just 5 days? = | = In 5 days, all fundraising will have to stop. In 5 days, I will focus entirely on passing meaningful legislation to make Maryland the best state it can be. Before the legislative session gavels in, we need to make the next 5 days count, friend. With that being said, we’re setting a $17,000 goal to help us hit the mark and start the session on a high note. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Elevate, Wes

name: Voter Action Project HQ
subject: As the new Congress is sworn in:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
With margins so tight in the House we have room to work, Team. We can stop these bills and more like them but we need to unite and fight now, before they get momentum and the House starts checking vote-suppressing bills off their to-do list
RUSH $25 or more right now as the new Congress starts to help defend our right to vote and stop opponents of voting rights in their tracks >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=010325_2&amount=25]
Voter Action Project Logo [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-weekly?refcode=010325_2&amount=25]
Truthout: Republicans Plan to Revive Legislative Attacks on Voting Rights [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=010325_2&amount=25]
This is the consequence of opponents of voting rights controlling both houses of Congress and the White House, folks:
A key House lawmaker recently made clear in an interview that the vote suppressors plan to push for a pair of their voting-related bills when they take control of both chambers of Congress and the White House this month. They plan to bring forward both the American Confidence in Elections (ACE) Act and the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act.
“ As we look to the new year with unified Republican government, we have a real opportunity to move these pieces of legislation not only out of committee, but across the House floor and into law, ” he said.
The ACE ACT " would nationalize harmful and unnecessary restrictions on voting rights, " while the SAVE Act -- which narrowly passed the House in 2024 and was stopped in the Senate -- requires proof of U.S. citizenship to vote in federal elections, which is something you already need, since non-citizen voting is illegal, and adds onerous verification requirements intended to drive off eligible voters.
Team, we have to be ready to stop these bills and more when the new Congress starts tomorrow. The margins are so thin and every bit helps as we hold our representatives accountable for their votes. Pitch in $25 or whatever you can today >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=010325_2&amount=25]

Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-weekly?refcode=010325_2&amount=25]

We can stop these bills and more like them but we need to unite and fight now, before they get momentum and the House starts checking vote-suppressing bills off their to-do list RUSH $25 or more right now as the new Congress starts to help defend our right to vote and stop opponents of voting rights in their tracks >> [] Voter Action Project Logo [] Truthout: Republicans Plan to Revive Legislative Attacks on Voting Rights [] This is the consequence of opponents of voting rights controlling both houses of Congress and the White House, folks: A key House lawmaker recently made clear in an interview that the vote suppressors plan to push for a pair of their voting-related bills when they take control of both chambers of Congress and the White House this month. They plan to bring forward both the American Confidence in Elections (ACE) Act and the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act. “ As we look to the new year with unified Republican government, we have a real opportunity to move these pieces of legislation not only out of committee, but across the House floor and into law, ” he said. The ACE ACT " would nationalize harmful and unnecessary restrictions on voting rights, " while the SAVE Act -- which narrowly passed the House in 2024 and was stopped in the Senate -- requires proof of U.S. citizenship to vote in federal elections, which is something you already need, since non-citizen voting is illegal, and adds onerous verification requirements intended to drive off eligible voters. Team, we have to be ready to stop these bills and more when the new Congress starts tomorrow. The margins are so thin and every bit helps as we hold our representatives accountable for their votes. Pitch in $25 or whatever you can today >> [] If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [] Other Amount [] [] [] [] [] [] Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country. Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities. Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? We don’t have a minute to waste. Virginia special elections are in just four days.
| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/99801819/520913640/-3547583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjQwYTdmMmI1LTA1Y2EtZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMjI0ODJhOWI0NSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=UkzKJJCQ3Cgra03JT_sYo4CgyhGghqOnfMTT2teyw6k=&emci=e247dd47-fdc9-ef11-88d0-0022482a9b45&emdi=40a7f2b5-05ca-ef11-88d0-0022482a9b45&ceid=8431045)
---

Peter, Happy New Year from the whole team at the NDRC! 2025 is going to be a big year in the long arc of our nation’s history. This is not an “off year,” and we don’t have a minute to waste. There’s a lot of important work ahead — and we’re already off to the races. **In just four days, Virginians head to the polls in legislative special elections. These races will decide the legislative majority in _both_ chambers.** We’ve endorsed three Democracy Defenders: **Jack Trammell** and **Kannan Srinivasan** for the Virginia Senate and **JJ Singh** for the Virginia House of Delegates. That’s not all: Later this year, voters will be choosing a state supreme justice in Wisconsin and three state supreme court justices in Pennsylvania. And of course, we need to be ready for the attacks on democracy from the Trump administration and his allies around the country. We already know they want to continue to blatantly gerrymander maps, make it harder for people to vote, and stack state supreme courts with extremist judges who rubberstamp manipulated maps. [**If you’re feeling motivated and energized for the work ahead, and you’re ready to kick off this new year on the right side of history, please send in a donation to the NDRC right now.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/99801820/520913641/1531424108?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_TYElectionYear_email_20250103-NDRC-Tier1-TYElectionYear_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250103-NDRC-Tier1-TYElectionYear_&utm_campaign=TYElectionYear&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjQwYTdmMmI1LTA1Y2EtZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMjI0ODJhOWI0NSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=UkzKJJCQ3Cgra03JT_sYo4CgyhGghqOnfMTT2teyw6k=) A new year always offers a bit of hope for what’s ahead. This year should be no different. Despite an incoming Trump administration, we _know_ we can be successful in protecting fair maps, combating ongoing gerrymandering, and more broadly defending our democracy — but only if we all come together on it. Thanks so much, NDRC | [DONATE](https://click.everyaction.com/k/99801821/520913642/1531424108?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_TYElectionYear_email_20250103-NDRC-Tier1-TYElectionYear_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250103-NDRC-Tier1-TYElectionYear_&utm_campaign=TYElectionYear&amount=30&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjQwYTdmMmI1LTA1Y2EtZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMjI0ODJhOWI0NSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=UkzKJJCQ3Cgra03JT_sYo4CgyhGghqOnfMTT2teyw6k=)

name: The Real Texas PAC
subject: Trump’s trifecta demands immediate action
body:
HELP DEMS WIN TWO HOUSE SEATS IN FLORIDA >>> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?refcode=111724_1]
Peter, we’ve just received breaking news out of Florida:
Governor Ron DeSantis has officially called on the Secretary of State to schedule TWO special elections on a RAPIDLY accelerated timeline.
To put it simply: Democrats now have a limited time to prepare to win TWO MORE HOUSE SEATS being vacated by Trump cabinet appointments
These special elections will determine the future of FL-01 , vacated by Matt Gaetz (reportedly stepping up as Trump’s pick for Attorney General), and FL-06 , soon to be left by Mike Waltz (rumored to become Trump’s National Security Advisor).
Flipping these suits blue IS possible - but only if we start preparing NOW. Will you help us build up a warchest of funding to rush to the Democrats who run for these seats? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?refcode=111724_1]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately: DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?express_lane=true&refcode=111724_1&amount=5]
DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?express_lane=true&refcode=111724_1&amount=10]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?express_lane=true&refcode=111724_1&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?express_lane=true&refcode=111724_1&amount=100]
DONATE $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?express_lane=true&refcode=111724_1&amount=250]
Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?refcode=111724_1&amount=25]
Make no mistake: DeSantis and his allies are trying to fast-track these elections to catch Democrats off guard.
They know they need to secure these seats quickly to maintain their MAGA control of Congress.
We CANNOT let them get away with it!
Chip in NOW so we can have a stash of funds ready to go when Democrats put up a candidate for these seats >>> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?refcode=111724_1]
Thanks for taking action,
Team RTP

Only U.S. citizens or legal permanent residents are eligible to contribute. An Individual can contribute up to $5,000 to The Real Texas PAC per calendar year. Married couples may together give a total of $10,000 per calendar year. Federal political action committees (PACs) can contribute up to $5,000 per calendar year. The first $5,000 contributed in a calendar year from federally permissible sources will be deposited in The Real Texas PAC's contribution account. Once a federally permissible source has contributed $5,000 in a calendar year, The Real Texas PAC will transfer any amounts in excess of $5,000 to its non-contribution account. All contributions from corporations and labor unions will be deposited in the non-contribution account. Please contact us at realtexas@mbacg.com if you would like a different allocation.

name: Rick Larsen
subject: Congress is back in session
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by Citizens to Elect Rick Larsen
body:
Today marks the first day of the 119th Congress; my priorities this year include:
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99801895/520913975/-201694504?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4N2MyYWVjNy0wNmNhLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTliNDUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9gB1ap1Oqrs5fcYfXSqWMMPTDrC0EK4g4dCgCju_Llo=&emci=92c3bf40-a2bd-ef11-88d0-000d3a9d5840&emdi=87c2aec7-06ca-ef11-88d0-0022482a9b45&ceid=9494239)
---

| | Friends — Today marks the first day of the 119th Congress, and my priorities this year remain focused on Northwest Washington. It is an honor to represent the 2nd District — one that I never take for granted. This Congress has the opportunity to cut through political divisions and address the concerns of hardworking Americans. I will lead by example as the top Democrat on the Transportation and Infrastructure Committee, and continue to prioritize bipartisan efforts and putting the American people first. **I am committed to protecting reproductive freedom, combatting fentanyl in our communities, building an economy that works for everyone, increasing access to health care, and supporting our veterans.** My colleagues and I in the 119th Congress have a lot of important work ahead of us. I am committed to working together where I can, resisting where I must, and making progress on the issues that matter most to you. clean_body: Today marks the first day of the 119th Congress; my priorities this year include: | | | | Friends — Today marks the first day of the 119th Congress, and my priorities this year remain focused on Northwest Washington. It is an honor to represent the 2nd District — one that I never take for granted. This Congress has the opportunity to cut through political divisions and address the concerns of hardworking Americans. I will lead by example as the top Democrat on the Transportation and Infrastructure Committee, and continue to prioritize bipartisan efforts and putting the American people first. I am committed to protecting reproductive freedom, combatting fentanyl in our communities, building an economy that works for everyone, increasing access to health care, and supporting our veterans. My colleagues and I in the 119th Congress have a lot of important work ahead of us. I am committed to working together where I can, resisting where I must, and making progress on the issues that matter most to you. More soon, Rick | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution to Rick Larsen instantly: | = | = | = | = ---|---|---|--- | | Paid for and authorized by Citizens to Elect Rick Larsen. | This email was sent by . name: EMILYs List
subject: [SURVEY] What do you want to see this year?
body:
We're finalizing our 2025 strategy and want to hear from you, Peter → |
---
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/99802050/520914560/-1060800536?refcode=WUENE102_20250103_2025SurveyRS_ALL&sourceid=1055547&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiM2U0Y2ZkMC0wN2NhLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTliNDUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NxI-W3UP1LauJ2ZxbwWff4wpIy8Qrb9QhUOBgpzHdhY=&emci=13e0f0c4-47c9-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&emdi=b3e4cfd0-07ca-ef11-88d0-0022482a9b45&ceid=5177934) Peter,

There's no such thing as an "off year" in politics. At EMILYs List, we know that every year is an opportunity to build the pro-choice movement and make sure we're growing a stronger, more inclusive Democratic Party. That's why we're already hard at work shaping our strategy for the year ahead — and we want YOU to be a part of it.

As we look at 2025 and beyond, we're committed to charting a new path forward, and your input is crucial. We're asking you to take a few minutes to complete our **2025 Priorities Survey** to help us understand what matters most to you. Your responses will help guide our efforts and ensure we're focusing on the issues that matter most to our supporters. | [TAKE THE SURVEY](https://click.everyaction.com/k/99802051/520914561/-1060800536?refcode=WUENE102_20250103_2025SurveyRS_ALL&sourceid=1055547&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiM2U0Y2ZkMC0wN2NhLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTliNDUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NxI-W3UP1LauJ2ZxbwWff4wpIy8Qrb9QhUOBgpzHdhY=&emci=13e0f0c4-47c9-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&emdi=b3e4cfd0-07ca-ef11-88d0-0022482a9b45&ceid=5177934)

clean_body: We're finalizing our 2025 strategy and want to hear from you, Peter → | Peter, There's no such thing as an "off year" in politics. At EMILYs List, we know that every year is an opportunity to build the pro-choice movement and make sure we're growing a stronger, more inclusive Democratic Party. That's why we're already hard at work shaping our strategy for the year ahead — and we want YOU to be a part of it. As we look at 2025 and beyond, we're committed to charting a new path forward, and your input is crucial. We're asking you to take a few minutes to complete our 2025 Priorities Survey to help us understand what matters most to you. Your responses will help guide our efforts and ensure we're focusing on the issues that matter most to our supporters. | It only takes a few minutes to complete the survey, but your voice will make a lasting difference. With your input and support, we can amplify our impact, support more candidates, and win even more critical elections in the years ahead! Peter,
I wanted to take a moment to express my gratitude for everything you’ve done to support our campaign. Thanks to your generosity, we were able to reach out to Minnesotans across the state and grow our movement in meaningful ways in 2024.
You also helped us meet a critical fundraising goal ahead of our first and only public reporting deadline of 2024. This is a huge statement as we prepare to fight to safeguard Minnesotans’ rights from Donald Trump and the extreme and dangerous agenda he plans to impose on Americans.
The new year is full of both possibilities and challenges. But I have faith that together, we will forge a path forward.
Thanks again and happy New Year,
Keith

subject: this is an email about our trauma
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Harry Dunn for Congress
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99802633/520916459/604658357?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.01.03_J6-Trauma_B1_F1_S1_C1_U2_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTAzODYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMTQ4YTg2ODItMGJjYS1lZjExLTg4ZDAtMDAyMjQ4MmE5YjQ1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=pBFQRVXd7rI-kJpMTAcpUZiyDYLer_8ioj9IPvt8zMM=#)
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99802634/520916460/-1139120540?amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.01.03_J6-Trauma_B1_F1_S1_C1_U2_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTAzODYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMTQ4YTg2ODItMGJjYS1lZjExLTg4ZDAtMDAyMjQ4MmE5YjQ1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=pBFQRVXd7rI-kJpMTAcpUZiyDYLer_8ioj9IPvt8zMM=) This is going to be a long email about our January 6th trauma.

[**_If you’re not ready to talk about it or don’t have time to read the whole email, I understand. But if you could still support Dunn's Democracy Defenders by making a small donation to help us hit our $15,000 goal, I’d appreciate it >>_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99802635/520916461/-1139120540?amount=10&amounts=10,25,50,100,250&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.01.03_J6-Trauma_B1_F1_S1_C1_U2_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTAzODYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMTQ4YTg2ODItMGJjYS1lZjExLTg4ZDAtMDAyMjQ4MmE5YjQ1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=pBFQRVXd7rI-kJpMTAcpUZiyDYLer_8ioj9IPvt8zMM=)

If you’re still reading this, I’m assuming you’re ready. Let’s talk about our trauma. You and me.

**January 6, 2021.**

The ripples from that day still threaten our democracy.

**_“Watch your back.” “I know where you sleep.” “Be afraid.”_**

Even worse? Donald Trump is threatening the January 6 committee members like Liz Cheney, Bennie Thompson and Adam Schiff.

Because I speak out about these challenges to our democracy, I get the same threatening messages. “We know where you live.”

The only difference between your January 6th trauma and mine is where we were when we experienced it.

I was at the Capitol immersed in a profane mix of sweat, screams, shrieks, anger, fear, blood, death, broken limbs, spit, hatred, horror, racism, bigotry, and heroism.

Capitol and Washington Police officers fought for our lives, the lives of fellow police officers, and the nation's elected leaders – it didn’t matter if they were a Republican, Democrat or an Independent. They were the men and women we sent to Washington to govern our nation.

**It was my duty to protect them. And our democracy.**

All the while, Donald Trump did _nothing_ to help us. For hours after inciting the violence himself – we were beaten bloody. And some died.

Even though my experience looks different from yours, I am first an American citizen. _A girl dad. A football lover. A voter. This is my country – _our country.__

That day was a stain on our nation. And we need to fight like hell to make sure we NEVER repeat a day like that. I’ll continue to use my voice to protect this country. I’ll stand up to the lies, hate, and bigotry. I will always stand my ground to make sure democracy exists.

[**_So I’m asking you to stand with me. We will get through this trauma – we will get through this nightmare – but only if we stand together. Can I count on you to contribute to our January 6th anniversary fund today?_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99802636/520916462/-1139120540?amount=10&amounts=10,25,50,100,250&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.01.03_J6-Trauma_B1_F1_S1_C1_U2_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTAzODYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMTQ4YTg2ODItMGJjYS1lZjExLTg4ZDAtMDAyMjQ4MmE5YjQ1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=pBFQRVXd7rI-kJpMTAcpUZiyDYLer_8ioj9IPvt8zMM=) | [$10](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99802637/520916463/-1139120540?amount=10&amounts=10,25,50,100,250&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.01.03_J6-Trauma_B1_F1_S1_C1_U2_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTAzODYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMTQ4YTg2ODItMGJjYS1lZjExLTg4ZDAtMDAyMjQ4MmE5YjQ1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=pBFQRVXd7rI-kJpMTAcpUZiyDYLer_8ioj9IPvt8zMM=) | [$25](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99802638/520916464/-1139120540?amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.01.03_J6-Trauma_B1_F1_S1_C1_U2_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTAzODYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMTQ4YTg2ODItMGJjYS1lZjExLTg4ZDAtMDAyMjQ4MmE5YjQ1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=pBFQRVXd7rI-kJpMTAcpUZiyDYLer_8ioj9IPvt8zMM=)

clean_body: | =# = This is going to be a long email about our January 6th trauma. = If you’re still reading this, I’m assuming you’re ready. Let’s talk about our trauma. You and me. January 6, 2021. The ripples from that day still threaten our democracy. “Watch your back.” “I know where you sleep.” “Be afraid.” Even worse? Donald Trump is threatening the January 6 committee members like Liz Cheney, Bennie Thompson and Adam Schiff. Because I speak out about these challenges to our democracy, I get the same threatening messages. “We know where you live.” The only difference between your January 6th trauma and mine is where we were when we experienced it. I was at the Capitol immersed in a profane mix of sweat, screams, shrieks, anger, fear, blood, death, broken limbs, spit, hatred, horror, racism, bigotry, and heroism. Capitol and Washington Police officers fought for our lives, the lives of fellow police officers, and the nation's elected leaders – it didn’t matter if they were a Republican, Democrat or an Independent. They were the men and women we sent to Washington to govern our nation. It was my duty to protect them. And our democracy. All the while, Donald Trump did nothing to help us. For hours after inciting the violence himself – we were beaten bloody. And some died. Even though my experience looks different from yours, I am first an American citizen. A girl dad. A football lover. A voter. This is my country – our country. That day was a stain on our nation. And we need to fight like hell to make sure we NEVER repeat a day like that. I’ll continue to use my voice to protect this country. I’ll stand up to the lies, hate, and bigotry. I will always stand my ground to make sure democracy exists. = | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Together, Harry 💙 = Harry Dunn for Congress PO Box 1449 Columbia, MD 21044 United States | Paid for by Harry Dunn for Congress

I was just sworn in to the Senate for my full 6-year term.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99803207/520918355/41798106?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzk3MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NzlhOTVhMC0wZGNhLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTliNDUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=7L7W2HIiFPzirTO_2YZoIFU7Ghq9sRMVmtOhI6IzWCw=&emci=c110bccc-dac9-ef11-88d0-0022482a9b45&emdi=479a95a0-0dca-ef11-88d0-0022482a9b45&ceid=26261389)

| | I was just sworn in to the Senate for my full 6-year term, and while I've already been working over the last few weeks in the Senate, I'm excited and grateful to begin the new session and work to get things done for New Jersey. New Jersey is the state that welcomed my two immigrant parents, the state that raised me, the state that gave me a chance to give back as a member of Congress representing the place where I grew up, and now the state **I will work every day to make proud as**** _your next United States Senator_****.** Now more than ever, our country needs hardworking, trustworthy leaders focused on the common good, bringing integrity into our politics, and bringing people together. Together, we can create an America where democracy, civility, trust, and respect flourish – and that's exactly what I aim to do in the Senate. As I said before, **I want to thank you for placing your trust in me to be New Jersey's next Senator. Whether you voted for me or not, you're my boss, and I'm here to serve you.** We have a lot of work to do, and as Trump takes control of the White House, we have a lot of dangerous proposals we're ready to fight against. I'm prepared to do whatever it takes to ensure New Jerseyans are given a fair chance and will fight for legislation that defends our American values and rights. Now, let's get to work for the people! Thank you, _Andy_ | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution to Andy Kim instantly:

| | **Thank You!** As I reflect on 2024, I am grateful for your trust and the outpouring support throughout the year. **THANK YOU!! We overcame obstacles, defended our seat, and are headed back to Washington for a second term.**

I am proud of the work we did together last year, and I am looking forward to all this year ahead holds.

**Now in 2025, it’s time to continue the work, fight for the American Dream, and deliver for Arizona's 6th congressional district.** _There is much more to do so let’s get to it 🇺🇸 _

>From Laura, me and our entire family: **THANK YOU, Happy New Year and I look forward to seeing you around the district - or DC - this year!**

Juan
